title: WorkOrder
description: The costable unit of work — one job against an asset, moving through a configurable lifecycle (see workflow-profile.schema.json and work-management-lifecycle doc 05). A work order reaches the machine either converted from a WorkRequest (enters at 'requested') or generated by a MaintenanceSchedule (enters at 'planned', strategy already approved). When generated it carries a content SNAPSHOT of the pinned job-plan version so history stays truthful after the plan later changes. One JSON document = one work order, embedding its tasks, labour, parts, failure report and audited transition history.

workOrderNumber.description: Human-facing sequential number, e.g. WO-2026-000123. Configurable numbering.

assetId.description: Primary asset the work is against. May be null for administrative/operations types whose WorkOrderType.requiresAsset is false.

workOrderTypeCode.description: The kind of work, referencing a WorkOrderType by code (PM/RM/AD/OP seeded). Schedule-generated work inherits the JobPlan.workOrderTypeCode; requested work is set on triage.

priority.description: Confirmed (authoritative) priority. Combines with asset criticality to drive scheduling order and escalation; suggested on the request, confirmed here.

status.description: Current lifecycle state — a state key from the WorkflowProfile this order follows (default seed: requested, reviewed, approved, planned, scheduled, assigned, in_progress, awaiting_parts_access, completed, verified, closed, rejected).

dueDate.description: Derived from priority + SLA target. Overdue work is a first-class dashboard/notification trigger.

workflowProfileKey.description: Key of the WorkflowProfile this order follows; null = the org default profile.

scheduleId.description: Provenance: the MaintenanceSchedule that generated this order (PM work). Null for requested/ad-hoc work.

workRequestId.description: Provenance: the WorkRequest this order was converted from. Null for schedule-generated/ad-hoc work.

jobPlanId.description: The pinned job plan (or class-family plan) this work follows. Null for ad-hoc work.

jobPlanVersion.description: The exact job-plan version pinned. With jobPlanId, identifies the content the snapshot was taken from.

parentWorkOrderId.description: For multi-stage or follow-up work discovered during execution.

dependsOn.description: Predecessor work-order ids that must complete first (scheduling dependencies).

assignedTo.description: Who the work is dispatched to.

estimatedLabourHours.description: Planned hours (from the plan or entered at planning).

actualLabourHours.description: Booked hours; derived as the sum of labour entries. Stored for convenience/reporting.

onHold.description: Present while in an on-hold state (awaiting_parts_access). Records why and pauses the SLA clock.

failure.description: Failure/cause/remedy coding, captured at close for reactive (RM) work whose type requiresFailureReport.

requiredPermits.description: Permits that must be issued before work starts (assigned -> in_progress guard).

statusHistory.description: The audited transition log — every state change, in order. The first entry records entry into the machine (from null). Closed/rejected entries are final.

rev.description: Optimistic-concurrency revision; bumped on every save.

snapshot.description: A frozen copy of the applicable content at generation time: the pinned job-plan version, resolved parameter values (from asset/model) and safety controls. This — not a live reference — is what the technician executes and what history keeps, so the record stays truthful after the plan, parameters or strategy later change.

parameters.description: Parameter values resolved from the asset/model at generation time (e.g. operating voltage, oil grade).

workOrderTask.description: An instantiated step / checklist item, copied from the pinned job-plan version (or ad-hoc). Carries both the definition and the captured result.

jobPlanTaskId.description: Origin task id in the pinned job-plan version; null for ad-hoc steps added on the order.

response.description: The captured result: boolean for passfail, number for numeric, string for text/choice, null if not yet done.

outOfRange.description: For a numeric response, whether it fell outside acceptableRange. An out-of-range/failed response may spawn a follow-up WorkRequest.

labourEntry.description: Time booked against the order — the basis of labour cost and utilisation.

partsUsage.description: Parts reserved then consumed against the order. Reserve-then-consume: reservedQty is set at 'scheduled', consumedQty deducts stock at or before 'completed' — never earlier.

partRef.description: Future reference to a Part; free text for now.

failureReport.description: Failure/cause/remedy coding — the raw material for reliability analysis (MTBF/MTTR).

statusEvent.description: One entry in the audited transition log.

from.description: Prior state key; null for the entry event.

reason.description: Required for rejections and holds; optional otherwise.
